title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Additional words: 1193
Multiple Term Insurance
What is Term Insurance? Can we have Multiple Term Insurance Policies?
A term insurance plan is a tool of financial protection. It offers unmatched coverage against the risk of premature death. The plan covers the life insured during the selected policy tenure. In the case of death during the tenure, a guaranteed death benefit is paid.
Term insurance plans have lower premiums so that you can choose optimal coverage and secure your family’s finances in your absence. The term plan is one of the preferred protection instruments because the policyholder can feel secure knowing that their family will be well looked after, even if they are no longer around due to unexpected reasons.
You can also avail protection from critical illnesses covered within the plan as well. You can ensure increased protection by opting for multiple add-on covers such as Accidental Death Benefit, Accidental Total Permanent Disability Benefit, and even Waiver of Premium Benefit, which can prove especially useful at times of financial difficulties.
Given the importance of term insurance plans and their comprehensive scope of protection, you can invest in multiple term insurance policies. Multiple plans would add different layers of protection to your finances and give 360-degree protection. Moreover, if the claim in any one-term plan is rejected, your family does not lose everything. Other plans might come to their rescue and give them the financial assistance that they need.
Common Reasons Why Term Insurance Claims Get Rejected
As stated earlier, multiple-term insurance plans come in handy if the claim is rejected in one plan. But why do claims get rejected? Here are some common reasons why –
Lapse of Policy
The lapse of the policy happens when you stop paying the premium. After you miss a premium payment, you are allowed a grace period to pay the outstanding premium. However, if you do not pay the premium even during the grace period, the policy lapses.
After the policy lapses, the coverage stops. If there is a claim in a lapsed policy, the claim would be rejected since the coverage has stopped.
Medical History and Lifestyle Habits Unknown to Insurer
If you have any medical history of lifestyle habits that the insurer does not know about and the death happens due to them, the claim would be rejected.
For instance, say the insured is a smoker, and the smoking habit is not known by the insurer. If the insured dies due to lung cancer due to smoking, a claim for the death would not be paid since the insurer did not know about the smoking habit of the insured.
Incorrect or Incomplete Information Provided to Insurer
You must provide correct and complete information about yourself, your medical and family history and your habits. If wrong or incomplete information is provided to the insurer and then the claim occurs due to such non-disclosure or misrepresentation, the insurer might reject the claim.
Non-Disclosure of Prior Insurance Policies
When you have existing insurance plans, you should inform your insurance company of the same. If the information is not provided, the insurer might reject your claim.
Claim due to an exclusion
Term insurance plans have a specified list of exclusions in which the coverage is not granted. These exclusions state the types of deaths that are not covered by the term insurance policy. If the death occurs due to the exclusion, the claim would not be paid.
For instance, term plans do not cover suicides within 12 months of buying the policy or reviving a lapsed policy. So, if the insured dies within 12 months of buying the plan, such a death would be excluded, and the claim would not be paid.
Pros of having multiple term insurance plans
There are multiple benefits of having multiple-term insurance plans. These benefits are as follows –
Longer tenures
You can buy different term insurance plans with different coverage tenures. This helps you get the benefit of differing maturities. While one plan might give coverage up to a certain age, the other might give coverage for an extended time. This helps you enjoy longer coverage tenures for maximum possible coverage.
For instance, say you buy one term plan when you are 25 years old. The term of the plan is 20 years. Five years later, you buy another term plan with a coverage term of 30 years. So, the first plan would grant coverage till you are 45 years old, while the second plan would cover you till 60 years of age. Thus, you can enjoy longer coverage when you have multiple policies.
Smaller Coverages
A high sum assured is needed so that the term plan can adequately secure your family’s finances. However, affording high coverage in one plan might prove unaffordable, especially when you are young and have limited income.
As such, with multiple plans, you can bring down the premium cost by opting for smaller coverages. Moreover, you can also opt for additional term plans when your income increases. This would enhance your coverage without pinching your pockets.
All in all, the aggregate coverage would be high while the aggregate premium would be low.
Reducing the risk of claim rejection
The risk of claim rejection is considerably reduced when you have multiple term insurance plans under your belt, as mentioned above.
The benefit of additional Riders
Some term plans come with inbuilt riders. Moreover, you can choose riders when you buy an additional plan. These riders are additional coverage features that offer additional protection and enhance the scope of your policy.
Improved benefits
With multiple-term insurance plans, your family can get higher benefits with multiple claims. This would give your family additional financial support and bolster their finances.
Increasing coverage with increasing needs
Every individual’s life is divided into different stages. At the changing stage of life, you might need additional coverage. In such cases, buying multiple policies help in meeting the need for higher coverage.
For instance, when you are unmarried, you can buy a term plan for securing the financial future of your dependent parents and other family members. However, after you get married, your coverage needs to increase and adding another term plan is better to increase your coverage. Again, after you become a parent, your coverage needs to increase. Then also, you can add another term plan to cater to the increased coverage needs.
Increased tax benefits
With multiple term insurance plans, the aggregate premium goes up. This allows you to claim a higher deduction under Section 80C, which allows deductions up to Rs.1.5 lakhs, subject to the limit as specified therein. A higher deductible means that you can bring down your tax liability, further helping you save more.
Steps to Buy or Claim Multiple Term Insurance Policies
Buying multiple-term insurance policies is easy. Here’s how you can do so –
Buy the term plan online or offline by filling up a proposal form and mentioning all your details.
Inform the new insurer about all existing insurance plans that you have
Submit your documents to the insurance company, and the policy will be issued
In the case of a claim, you just have to inform the insurer for claim settlement. The steps are as follows –
The nominee would have to inform all the insurance companies about the death of the insured.
The claim form should be filled out, and the death certificate should be submitted.
In the case of unnatural or accidental death, a police FIR and additional documents like the panchnama, coroner’s report, hospital records, etc. would also be needed.
The nominee should also submit his identity proof and bank details
The insurance company would verify the documents and settle the claim.
Conclusion
When it comes to buying term insurance plans, the more, the merrier! Multiple term insurance plans help in providing additional coverage and benefits. Moreover, you can save taxes, too and enjoy comprehensive coverage at affordable premiums. So, for your increasing coverage needs, opt for multiple-term insurance plans and provide complete financial security to your family.
